info.title: bookkeeprr API
info.description:
Native REST API plus the Readarr-compatible adapter (`/api/readarr/v1/*`),
which maps bookkeeprr resources to Readarr shapes for compat clients.

## Authentication

Send **any one** of the supported credentials — they are alternatives, not
requirements. Both work on the native surface **and** on the
Readarr-compatible surface:

- **Bearer token** — a personal API key (`Authorization: Bearer bkr_…`),
  created under **Account → API keys**. Acts as the owning user. The
  recommended mode for scripts and API clients.
- **`X-Api-Key` header** — the static system key, generated under
  **Settings → API Access**. The conventional mode for Readarr-compat
  clients (Calibre-Web etc.), which usually only know how to send
  `X-Api-Key`.

Requests without a valid credential get **401**. `/api/health`,
`/api/first-run/*`, and `/api/auth/*` stay open.

## Conventions

- Responses are always JSON; send `Content-Type: application/json` on writes.
- Errors return `{"error": "human message"}`, sometimes with `detail`
  (underlying error) or `hint` (recovery suggestion). The Readarr-compatible
  surface uses Readarr's own envelope instead: `{"message", "description?"}`.
- Status codes: `200` success · `201` created · `202` long-running job
  enqueued · `204` no content (deletes) · `400` malformed body or id ·
  `404` not found · `409` conflict (duplicate, already grabbed, job in
  progress) · `422` referenced row does not exist · `502` upstream service
  failure · `503` required service not configured.
- Settings endpoints never return stored secrets — reads are masked
  (`"****"` or `"••••••••"`), and writing the mask or an empty string keeps
  the stored value. Per-field semantics are documented on each schema.

servers.description: Your self-hosted bookkeeprr instance — substitute your own host.

get.summary: List series, paginated, with read-state/health enrichment

post.description: The body is a union discriminated on `contentType`. Most branches return the full created series row; the `light_novel` branch returns just `{ id }`. Every branch accepts an optional `groupId` to file the series under a library group (422 when the group does not exist).

groupId.description: Library group to file the new series under. 422 when the group does not exist.
Library group to file the new series under. 422 when the group does not exist.
Library group to file the new series under. 422 when the group does not exist.
Library group to file the new series under. 422 when the group does not exist.
Library group to file the new series under. 422 when the group does not exist.
Library group to file the new series under. 422 when the group does not exist.

get.description: Federated metadata search; the provider is selected by `contentType` (AniList, ComicVine, OpenLibrary, Audnex). Manga responses use `hits`; the other content types use `results`. 503 only on `contentType=comic` when ComicVine is not configured.

post.description: Searches manga only (AniList with MangaDex completion fallback). Prefer `GET /api/series/search?contentType=manga`.

get.description: Returns the full series row plus enrichment fields (`title`, `monitored`, `volumes`, `downloaded`, `groupPath`, `volumesList`). Also includes `hydrating: true` while any background job (metadata/volume hydrate, chapter sync, import) is still active for this series. Clients should poll until `hydrating` is false to pick up freshly-enriched data.

patch.summary: Update monitoring/root/profile fields; move between library groups
patch.description: `groupId` moves the series into a library group (`null` ungroups); 422 when the group does not exist.

groupId.description: Move the series into a library group (`null` to ungroup). 422 when the group does not exist.

delete.description: Cascades to volumes, chapters, releases, downloads, and library-file rows. Files on disk are not touched.

get.description: Annotated with ownership (`none` | `in-library` | `downloading`) and the source indexer. Capped at the 200 most-recent rows.

post.summary: Grab a user-supplied magnet/torrent for the series
post.description: Accepts JSON `{ magnet }` or a multipart form upload with a `torrent` file field (max 2 MiB). 409 when the torrent is already active or imported; 503 when qBittorrent is unconfigured; 502 when the qBittorrent add fails.

post.summary: Interactive indexer search for a series
post.description: Forces a fresh poll of every enabled indexer covering the series content type (doesn't use cached releases). Matching results are upserted into the releases table as a side effect, so subsequent grabs work. Results are sorted matches-first (score desc), then by seeders. 502 only when every indexer failed AND zero items were collected.

Grabs a result straight from an interactive search — including non-matching results, which have no release row yet. Pass the `item` and `parsed` objects back verbatim from the search response; the route upserts the release row and sends it to qBittorrent. 409 when the release already has an active download; 503 when qBittorrent is unconfigured; 502 when the qBittorrent add fails.

Sends the release to qBittorrent and inserts a download row. No request body. 409 when the release already has an active download; 503 when qBittorrent is unconfigured; 502 when the add fails or the hash never appears in the qBittorrent category.

get.description: Activity feed: download rows joined with their release + series, newest first, capped at 200. Active rows are enriched best-effort with live qBittorrent transfer stats (progress/speed/ETA/seeds) — null when qBittorrent is unconfigured or unreachable.

delete.description: Admin only. Deletes all terminal download rows (completed / imported / failed); active rows are kept. Error responses use the `{ message }` envelope.

Admin only. Cancels a download: removes the torrent (with files) from qBittorrent AND deletes the download row. Both steps are best-effort and idempotent — a failed qBittorrent delete still clears the row (200). Error responses use the `{ message }` envelope.

post.description: Admin only. Pauses the torrent in qBittorrent. 502 when qBittorrent is unconfigured or the call fails. Error responses use the `{ message }` envelope.
Admin only. Resumes the torrent in qBittorrent. 502 when qBittorrent is unconfigured or the call fails. Error responses use the `{ message }` envelope.

Admin only. Pauses every torrent across the per-content-type 'bookkeeprr-*' qBittorrent categories. 502 when qBittorrent is unconfigured or the call fails. Error responses use the `{ message }` envelope.

get.description: Admin only. Each row carries its per-kind config as a JSON-encoded string in `configJson`; secrets within (filelist `passkey`, torznab `apiKey`) come back masked to "". The internal "manual" sentinel indexer (hand-added torrents) is never listed. Error responses use the `{ message }` envelope.

configJson.description: JSON-encoded per-kind config object. Secrets within (filelist `passkey`, torznab `apiKey`, mam `mamId`) are masked to "".

post.description: Admin only. `kind` must match `configJson.kind` (mismatch → 400); a body that fails schema validation returns 422. 401/403 use the `{ message }` envelope.

patch.description: Admin only. `configJson.kind` must match the existing row (sending a filelist body to a nyaa row returns 400). Blank secrets ("" for filelist `passkey` / torznab `apiKey`) leave the stored value unchanged — the GET route masks secrets to "", so a save that did not re-enter one keeps the stored secret. 401/403 use the `{ message }` envelope.

passkey.description: Masked to "" on GET. Send "" to leave the stored passkey unchanged; send a real value to rotate it.

apiKey.description: Masked to "" on GET. Send "" to leave the stored API key unchanged; send a real value to rotate it.

mamId.description: Masked to "" on GET. Send "" to leave the stored session unchanged; send a real value to rotate it.

delete.description: Admin only. 401/403 use the `{ message }` envelope.

post.description: Admin only. When the body carries both `url` and `apiKey` the connection is persisted first; otherwise the stored connection is used. Mirrors every Prowlarr indexer as a managed torznab row (adds/updates/disables). 502 when Prowlarr is unreachable or not configured. 401/403 use the `{ message }` envelope.

Admin only. Blank/absent `url`/`apiKey` fall back to the stored connection so a test works without re-entering the masked key; 400 when neither the body nor the store yields both. 502 when the connection test fails. 401/403 use the `{ message }` envelope.

Admin only. Fetches `t=caps` from the endpoint and returns its category tree. Send `apiKey: ""` plus `indexerId` to fall back to that row's stored key (the edit form masks it); 400 when no key is available. 502 when the probe fails. 401/403 use the `{ message }` envelope.

apiKey.description: Send "" together with `indexerId` to fall back to that row's stored API key (the edit form masks it).

get.description: Returns `{ total, monitored, missing }` series counts. Bearer-only: requires a mobile API token (`Authorization: Bearer …`); session cookies are not accepted on this endpoint.

missing.description: Monitored series whose totalVolumes is set and whose imported volume-level file count is below it. Approximation: chapter-granularity series and series without totalVolumes are counted as not missing.

post.description: Admin only. Enqueues a background `library_health_scan` job that opens every library file with the reader probers and deletes / re-grabs corrupt or wrong-format content. Poll `GET /api/jobs/{jobId}` for progress. 409 (with the existing job id) when a health scan is already pending/running. 401/403 use the `{ message }` envelope.

get.description: Admin only. Computes the rename plan for every series and returns only those with pending changes; nothing is written to disk. Series whose plan cannot be computed are skipped from the preview. 401/403 use the `{ message }` envelope.

totalChanges.description: Folder renames + file renames across all series.

post.description: Admin only. Enqueues a background `library_rename_all` job that re-applies the naming templates to every series on disk. Poll `GET /api/jobs/{jobId}` for progress. 401/403 use the `{ message }` envelope.

get.description: Every group with its display `path` (ancestor names joined with `' / '`), RECURSIVE `seriesCount` (members of subgroups count into the ancestor), and direct-children `subgroupCount`.

post.description: Admin only. Omit `parentId` for a root group. Sibling names must be unique — 409 on a conflict; 422 when `parentId` does not exist. 401/403 use the `{ message }` envelope.

patch.description: Admin only. Send `name` and/or `parentId` (`parentId: null` moves the group to the root). 409 on a sibling-name conflict; 422 when the reparent would create a cycle or the group/parent does not exist. 401/403 use the `{ message }` envelope.

delete.description: Admin only. **Recursive cascade**: deletes the group, every subgroup beneath it, AND every member series record — each series goes through the regular series-delete path, so its volumes, files, and downloads cascade exactly like a manual delete. **Disk files are untouched.** Returns the deleted-group and deleted-series counts. 422 on an unknown id. 401/403 use the `{ message }` envelope.

post.description: Moves an existing library file to a different series and volume/chapter, renaming it on disk with the current naming template. Exactly one of `volumeNumber` / `chapterNumber` must be sent. 409 when the destination path already exists.

post.summary: Scan library roots for untracked files and suggest metadata matches
post.description: Admin only. Scans every configured library root for files not yet tracked as library_file rows, then queries OpenLibrary and Google Books for each found item in parallel (capped at 8 concurrent items). Returns the full matched-item list for the import grid. Provider failures are swallowed — `best` is null and `alternatives` is empty when no match is found. 401/403 use the `{ message }` envelope.

path.description: Absolute path to the file (PER_FILE types) or directory (others).

detectedTitle.description: Title inferred from the filename / directory name.

files.description: Absolute paths of the individual media files in this item.

sizeBytes.description: Total size in bytes across all files.

best.description: Top-ranked metadata candidate, or null when no providers returned results.

alternatives.description: Up to 4 alternative candidates ranked below best.

post.summary: Adopt confirmed import rows into the library
post.description: Admin only. Accepts a list of confirmed import rows (each pairing an on-disk item with a chosen metadata candidate), then creates (or reuses) the series record and inserts a library_file row per file. Fully idempotent — re-running the same rows creates 0 new rows. Rows whose content type is unsupported for direct import (manga/comic) or that fail for any reason are skipped rather than aborting the batch — they appear in the `skipped` array. Returns the number of newly inserted library_file rows, the deduplicated series ids touched, and any skipped rows. 401/403 use the `{ message }` envelope.

path.description: Absolute path to the file (PER_FILE types) or directory (others).

detectedTitle.description: Title inferred from the filename / directory name.

files.description: Absolute paths of the individual media files in this item.

sizeBytes.description: Total size in bytes across all files.

monitor.description: When true, monitoring is set to "all"; false → "none".

imported.description: Number of new library_file rows created across all rows.

seriesIds.description: Deduplicated list of series ids touched.

reason.description: Human-readable reason the row was skipped.

skipped.description: Rows that could not be adopted (unsupported content type or transient error).

get.description: Returns all book series, optionally filtered by `contentType` (`ebook` | `audiobook`). Each entry includes `memberCount` (the number of library series linked to this book series). 400 when `contentType` is not a valid book-series content type.

post.description: Admin only. Creates a book series with `contentType` restricted to `ebook` or `audiobook`. `source` is set to `manual`; `memberCount` in the response is always `0` at creation time. 400 on body validation failure. 401/403 use the `{ message }` envelope.

get.summary: Book series detail with merged books list
get.description: Returns full detail for a book series. The `books` array merges owned library members with unmatched saga entries: entries matched by `externalRef` (isbn/asin) or title+position become owned entries; members with no matching entry are appended as owned orphans. The list is sorted by position (nulls last).

patch.description: Admin only. Updates `name`, `description`, and/or `coverUrl`. At least one field is required. 401/403 use the `{ message }` envelope.

delete.description: Admin only. Removes the book series, its member links, and its saga entries. The linked library series themselves are not deleted. 401/403 use the `{ message }` envelope.

post.summary: Assign a library series to a book series
post.description: Admin only. Idempotent upsert: re-assigning a series that is already a member updates its position and preserves the `manual` linkSource — never returns 409. Returns the refreshed detail on success. 422 when the library series does not exist or its content type does not match the book series. 401/403 use the `{ message }` envelope.

delete.summary: Unassign a library series from a book series
delete.description: Admin only. Removes the member link between a library series and this book series. Idempotent: deleting a series that is not a member is a no-op and returns 200. 401/403 use the `{ message }` envelope.

post.summary: Trigger detection refresh for a book series (scaffold)
post.description: Admin only. Enqueues a background detection refresh for the book series. Returns 202 immediately. NOTE: detection job wiring lands in a later task — this endpoint is a scaffold that accepts and acknowledges the request without enqueuing yet. 401/403 use the `{ message }` envelope.

Enqueues a `library_scan` job over `rootPath`. Poll `GET /api/jobs/{jobId}` until the job is terminal, then review the unmatched groups via `GET /api/scan/groups`. Optional `targetGroupId` + `structure` ('flat' default | 'mirror') ride the scan session through to confirm: newly created series are filed into the target group (flat) or into groups mirroring the folder tree under it (mirror); pre-existing matched series keep their group. 400 when `rootPath` is missing or not readable; 409 (with the existing job id) when a library scan is already pending/running; 422 when `targetGroupId` does not exist.

rootPath.description: Absolute directory to scan; must be readable.

targetGroupId.description: Library group newly imported series are filed into at confirm time. Omit for the library root. 422 when the group does not exist.

structure.description: Import structure (default 'flat'). flat: every confirmed match gets groupId = targetGroupId. mirror: the series directory's path relative to the scan root — minus the series folder itself — materializes as nested groups under the target; series folders directly at the scan root land in the target group itself. Only NEWLY created series are assigned — pre-existing matched series keep their group.

get.description: Pending scan matches grouped by directory (one group per directory), sorted by `dirname`. Each group carries the proposed AniList match, average parser confidence, per-file parsed metadata, and `relativeDir` (the series dir relative to the scan root — the mirror import preview; '' for root-level dirs or legacy rows). Paths are absolute host paths — scrub the response if proxying to less-trusted users.

dirHash.description: Stable hash of the directory path — the {dirHash} path param of the group routes.

directory.description: Absolute filesystem path on the host.

existingSeriesId.description: Set when a series with the proposed AniList id is already in the library.

inferredGranularity.description: 'chapter' when any file in the group parsed as a chapter.

relativeDir.description: Series directory relative to the scan root that produced the group, e.g. 'Shonen/Vinland Saga'. '' when the directory sits at the scan root or the rows predate scan-session params.

structure.description: The scan session's import structure (see POST /api/scan). null when the scan omitted it (flat default) or the rows predate scan-session params.

path.description: Absolute filesystem path on the host.

post.summary: Attach an AniList match to a group
post.description: Manual fix-up: looks up `anilistId` on AniList and stamps the match onto every pending row in the group (overriding the parser proposal). 502 when the AniList lookup fails.

No request body. Imports the group into the library: creates the series if its AniList match is not already present (then enqueues metadata-hydrate jobs) and registers a library-file row per file; files already in the library count into `skippedCount`. A NEWLY created series is filed per the scan session's `targetGroupId` / `structure` (mirror materializes the folder chain between scan root and series dir as nested groups, find-or-create per segment); pre-existing matched series keep their group. 400 when the group has no match attached; 404 when the group is unknown or already resolved.

skippedCount.description: Files skipped because a library file with the same path already exists.

post.description: No request body. Marks every pending row in the group rejected so it no longer appears in `GET /api/scan/groups`.

get.description: Poll a background job. The row is returned as-is: `payloadJson` / `resultJson` are JSON-encoded strings; timestamps are ISO strings.

resultJson.description: JSON-encoded job result, set on completion.

post.description: Admin only. Drains all pending jobs of the given kind through the runner. Self-enqueueable kinds (`qbt_watch`, `housekeeping`) get a fresh empty-payload job enqueued first so this works as a "run it now" trigger; `import` and `library_scan` only drain what something else already enqueued (`ran: 0` when idle). 401/403 use the `{ message }` envelope.

get.description: Returns the array directly (not wrapped). `preferredGroupsJson` / `preferredLanguagesJson` are JSON-encoded string arrays.

post.summary: Make a quality profile the default
post.description: Admin only. Clears the default flag on every other profile and sets it on `id`, atomically; returns the updated row. All error responses on this endpoint (400/401/403/404) use the `{ message }` envelope.

get.description: Every volume with a known release date inside the `[from, to)` window (`to` is EXCLUSIVE), sorted by date, then series title, then volume number. Dates are YYYY-MM-DD interpreted as UTC midnight. 400 when either bound is malformed or `to` is not after `from`.

schema.description: Window start (inclusive), YYYY-MM-DD, interpreted as UTC midnight.
Window end (EXCLUSIVE), YYYY-MM-DD. Must be after `from`.

seriesTitle.description: First non-null of english/romaji/native; falls back to `Series #<id>`.

get.description: The stored password is masked to "****" ("" when unset).

password.description: "****" when a password is stored, "" otherwise.

put.description: Admin only. Full replace. `password: ""` keeps the stored password (the GET route masks it); there is no null-clear.

password.description: Masked to "****" on GET ("" when unset). Send "" to keep the stored password; send a real value to rotate. There is no null-clear.

get.summary: Naming templates for a content type
get.description: Returns only the template keys valid for the content type (e.g. `chapter` only where chapters exist). `contentType` defaults to `manga` for back-compat.

put.summary: Save naming templates for a content type
put.description: Admin only. Only the keys valid for the content type are applied; an empty `volume_subfolder` flattens. Template-validation failures return 400 with `error` and the failing `position`.

get.description: The stored key is masked to "****" ("" when unset).

apiKey.description: "****" when a key is stored, "" otherwise.

put.description: Admin only. `apiKey: ""` keeps the stored key; there is no null-clear.

apiKey.description: Masked to "****" on GET ("" when unset). Send "" to keep the stored key; send a real value to rotate. There is no null-clear.

get.description: The stored key is masked to "****" ("" when unset).

apiKey.description: "****" when a key is stored, "" otherwise.

put.summary: Save the Google Books API key
put.description: Admin only. `apiKey: ""` keeps the stored key; there is no null-clear.

apiKey.description: Masked to "****" on GET ("" when unset). Send "" to keep the stored key; send a real value to rotate. There is no null-clear.

get.description: The stored Client ID is masked to "****" ("" when unset).

clientId.description: "****" when a Client ID is stored, "" otherwise.

put.description: Admin only. `clientId: ""` (or the literal "****") keeps the stored value; there is no null-clear.

clientId.description: Masked to "****" on GET ("" when unset). Send "" or "****" to keep the stored Client ID; send a real value to rotate. There is no null-clear.

post.description: Admin only. Runs a probe search. Omit `clientId` to test the stored one; sending one tests it without persisting. 502 with `{ ok: false, error }` on failure.

clientId.description: Omit to test the stored Client ID.

get.description: The stored key is masked to "****" ("" when unset).

apiKey.description: "****" when a key is stored, "" otherwise.

put.summary: Save the NYT Books API key
put.description: Admin only. `apiKey: ""` (or the literal "****") keeps the stored key; there is no null-clear.

apiKey.description: Masked to "****" on GET ("" when unset). Send "" or "****" to keep the stored key; send a real value to rotate. There is no null-clear.

post.summary: Test the NYT Books API key
post.description: Admin only. Fetches the audio bestsellers list as a probe. Omit `apiKey` to test the stored one. 502 with `{ ok: false, error }` on failure.

apiKey.description: Omit to test the stored API key.

get.description: The stored API key is masked to "****" ("" when unset).

apiKey.description: "****" when a key is stored, "" otherwise.

put.description: Admin only. `apiKey: ""` (or the literal "****") keeps the stored key; `url` is always applied. There is no null-clear.

apiKey.description: Masked to "****" on GET ("" when unset). Send "" or "****" to keep the stored key; send a real value to rotate. There is no null-clear.

get.description: Not a secret — the URL round-trips unmasked. "" means disabled.

post.description: Admin only. Solves a Cloudflare-protected probe page through the endpoint. Omit `url` to test the stored one. 502 with `{ ok: false, error }` on failure (including "not configured").

url.description: Omit to test the stored URL.

put.description: Admin only. Strict full replace — every provider key is required and unknown keys are rejected.

get.description: Admin only. Per-content-type library roots / qBittorrent categories, the torrent-cleanup policy, and the cover image cache.

put.description: Admin only. `imageCache` is optional (older clients); schema failures return 422.

get.description: Webhook URLs are masked to "••••••••" (null when unset); the `*Configured` booleans report whether each transport is set up.

discordWebhookUrl.description: "••••••••" when configured, null otherwise (never the real URL).

appriseUrl.description: "••••••••" when configured, null otherwise (never the real URL).

patch.description: Admin only. For the webhook fields, "" keeps the stored value and null clears it.

discordWebhookUrl.description: Masked to "••••••••" on GET. "" keeps the stored URL; null clears it.

discordAvatarUrl.description: "" keeps the stored URL; null clears it.

appriseUrl.description: Masked to "••••••••" on GET. "" keeps the stored URL; null clears it.

post.description: No request body. Fires a synthetic event through every configured transport; always 200 with a per-transport result ("ok" | "not-configured" | `{ error }`).

get.description: The stored API token is masked to "••••••••" (null when unset).

apiToken.description: "••••••••" when a token is stored, null otherwise (never the real token).

patch.description: Admin only. For `baseUrl` / `apiToken` / `libraryId`: "" keeps the stored value, null clears it.

baseUrl.description: "" keeps the stored value; null clears it.

apiToken.description: Masked to "••••••••" on GET. "" keeps the stored token; null clears it.

libraryId.description: "" keeps the stored value; null clears it.

post.description: No request body. Triggers a library scan on the configured server. 503 when Audiobookshelf is not configured; 502 (with `{ error }`) when the scan call fails.

get.description: For the library-picker dropdown. 503 when Audiobookshelf is not configured; 502 (with `{ error }`) when the listing fails.
The stored password is masked to "••••••••" (null when unset).

password.description: "••••••••" when a password is stored, null otherwise (never the real password).

patch.description: Admin only. For `baseUrl` / `username` / `password`: "" keeps the stored value, null clears it.

baseUrl.description: "" keeps the stored value; null clears it.

username.description: "" keeps the stored value; null clears it.

password.description: Masked to "••••••••" on GET. "" keeps the stored password; null clears it.

post.description: No request body. Triggers a content-server library refresh. 503 when Calibre is not configured; 502 (with `{ error }`, e.g. "unsupported-version" for content-servers older than v6) when the refresh fails.

get.description: Unlike the other settings secrets, the key is returned in PLAINTEXT when enabled (the admin UI shows it for copy/paste); "" when disabled.

key.description: The actual key (plaintext) when enabled, "" when disabled.

patch.summary: Generate or disable the API key
patch.description: Admin only. `action: "generate"` rotates and returns the new plaintext key; `action: "disable"` clears it (every /api/* request becomes open again).

post.description: No request body — send the candidate key as the `X-Api-Key` header. When no key is configured the response carries a `note` saying auth is disabled. 401 `{ ok: false, error }` on mismatch.

get.description: Admin only. Returns the config blob bare (currently `{ dryRun }`).

patch.description: Admin only. Strict partial merge; schema failures return 422.

get.description: Admin only. All four retention blobs (jobs / backups / visibility / releases); each is written via its own PATCH subroute.

patch.description: Admin only. Strict partial merge; schema failures return 422.
Admin only. Strict partial merge; schema failures return 422.
Admin only. Strict partial merge; schema failures return 422.
Admin only. Strict partial merge; schema failures return 422.
Admin only. Strict partial merge; schema failures return 422.

get.description: Admin only. Scoring weights + the adult-content filter.

patch.description: Admin only. Strict partial merge; schema failures return 422. When "auto-replay on save" is enabled, the response also reports the enqueued replay run (or its error).
Admin only. Strict partial merge; schema failures return 422. When "auto-replay on save" is enabled, the response also reports the enqueued replay run (or its error).
Admin only. Schema failures return 422.

post.description: Probes the qBittorrent WebUI with the submitted connection. `password` blank/absent falls back to the stored password (the settings GET masks it). 502 with `{ ok: false, error }` on failure.

password.description: Blank/absent falls back to the stored password.

post.description: Validates the key against ComicVine. `apiKey` blank/absent falls back to the stored key; 400 when neither yields one. 502 with `{ ok: false, error }` on failure.

apiKey.description: Blank/absent falls back to the stored key.

post.description: Only works while ZERO users exist (409 afterwards). The email doubles as the username. Sets the session cookie on success.

password.description: Must satisfy the password policy (min 8 chars).

post.description: Accepts JSON or a classic form POST. Two 200 shapes: `{ user, … }` (session cookie set) or `{ requiresTotp, challengeToken }` when the account has 2FA — complete via POST /api/auth/login/totp. Mobile onboarding: a `bookkeeprr://` `return_to` yields `redirect_to` with a one-time exchange code (non-JSON form posts get a real 302 instead). 400 is `{ message }` for body errors but `{ error }` for a bad `return_to` scheme.

return_to.description: Mobile onboarding only — MUST be a `bookkeeprr://` URL. On success the response carries `redirect_to` with a one-time exchange code appended (or, for non-JSON form posts, a real 302 to it).

redirect_to.description: Only when a valid `return_to` was sent — `bookkeeprr://…?exchange=…`.

post.description: Exchanges the `challengeToken` from POST /api/auth/login plus a 6-digit TOTP code (or a `xxxx-xxxx-xxxx` recovery code, consumed on use) for a session cookie.

code.description: A 6-digit TOTP code OR a `xxxx-xxxx-xxxx` recovery code (consumed on use).

redirect_to.description: Only when a valid `return_to` was sent — `bookkeeprr://…?exchange=…`.

post.description: Revokes the current session (if any) and clears the cookie. Always 204 — safe to call without a session.
Session-cookie only. Revokes EVERY session of the current user (including this one) and clears the cookie.

get.description: Probe endpoint: returns `{ user }` with the session user when authenticated, or `{ user: null }` with HTTP 200 (never 401) when there is no valid session — safe to call without credentials. `totpEnabledAt` is epoch milliseconds, unlike the ISO timestamps elsewhere.

totpEnabledAt.description: Epoch milliseconds (NOT an ISO string); null when 2FA is off.

delete.description: Session-cookie only; local accounts only (OIDC/forward-auth → 400). Requires the current password; cascades sessions and per-user data and clears the cookie.

patch.description: Session-cookie only. "" clears a field (stored as null).

email.description: "" clears; otherwise must be a valid email.

get.description: Session-cookie only. Creates the row with defaults on first read.

patch.description: Session-cookie only. Strict partial merge (unknown keys → 400).

get.description: Any user credential works (cookie, bearer, personal API key); the X-Api-Key "system" actor is rejected. Secrets are never re-shown — only the display prefix.

keyPrefix.description: First 8 chars of the random part, for display.

lastUsedAt.description: ISO timestamp; null when never used.

post.description: Returns the full `bkr_…` key in `plaintext` ONCE; only the prefix is stored for display. Use it as `Authorization: Bearer bkr_…`.

plaintext.description: The full `bkr_…` key — shown only in this response.

post.description: Local accounts only (400 otherwise). Generates a secret + recovery codes WITHOUT persisting — enable via POST /api/auth/me/totp/enable.
Echo the /setup payload back with a valid current code. 422 when the code does not verify against the secret.

delete.description: Requires the current local password (401 on mismatch). Clears the secret and recovery codes.

post.description: Requires the current local password and 2FA enabled. Returns 10 fresh plaintext codes (shown once); the old codes stop working.
Session-cookie only; local accounts only. `currentPassword` is required unless the account is flagged `mustChangePassword`. Revokes every session and issues a fresh cookie for this client.

get.description: Session-cookie only. `id` is the first 12 chars of each session token — the handle for DELETE /api/auth/sessions/{tokenPrefix}.

id.description: First 12 chars of the session token — the revoke handle.

current.description: True for the session making this request.

delete.description: Session-cookie only. 400 when the prefix matches the CURRENT session (use logout); 409 when the prefix is ambiguous — send more characters.

get.description: For the login page: whether SSO is enabled (and configured) plus the button label. No secrets.

enabled.description: True only when OIDC is enabled AND fully configured.

get.description: Admin only. `clientSecret` is masked to "••••••••" ("" when unset).

clientSecret.description: Masked to "••••••••" on GET ("" when unset). On PATCH: "" keeps the stored secret, null clears it (and force-disables OIDC), a real value rotates it.

patch.description: Admin only. Partial merge. For `clientSecret`: "" keeps the stored secret, null clears it AND force-disables OIDC, a real value rotates.

clientSecret.description: "" keeps the stored secret; null clears it AND disables OIDC.
Masked to "••••••••" on GET ("" when unset). On PATCH: "" keeps the stored secret, null clears it (and force-disables OIDC), a real value rotates it.

post.description: Admin only. Runs issuer discovery with the submitted (or stored) client secret; blank/masked secret falls back to the stored one. 502 with `{ ok: false, error: "discovery_failed", detail }` on failure.

clientSecret.description: Blank/absent/masked ("••••••••") falls back to the stored secret.

get.description: Admin only. Nothing here is a secret — round-trips unmasked.

trustedProxies.description: CIDR list; every entry is validated on PATCH.

patch.description: Admin only. Partial merge. 422 on invalid CIDRs, or — when flipping `enabled` on — when the CURRENT request does not already arrive via a trusted proxy with the user header set (lockout guard; the 422 body is the readiness report).

trustedProxies.description: CIDR list; every entry is validated on PATCH.
CIDR list; every entry is validated on PATCH.

get.description: Admin only. Full user rows minus the password hash; the avatar is exposed as `avatarUrl`.

lastLoginAt.description: ISO timestamp; null when never logged in.

totpEnabledAt.description: ISO timestamp; null when 2FA is off.

post.description: Admin only. `mustChangePassword` defaults to true (the user is forced to set their own password on first login). 409 on a duplicate username. Unlike the list view, the created row carries the raw `avatarPath` (null) instead of `avatarUrl`.

password.description: Must satisfy the password policy (min 8 chars).

lastLoginAt.description: ISO timestamp; null when never logged in.

totpEnabledAt.description: ISO timestamp; null when 2FA is off.

patch.description: Admin only. Disabling revokes all of the target’s sessions. 409 guards: cannot demote or disable the last active admin; cannot disable your own account.

disabled.description: Disabling revokes all of the user’s sessions.

delete.description: Admin only. 409 guards: cannot delete your own account or the last active admin.

post.description: Admin only. Sets the new password, revokes all of the target’s sessions, and (by default) forces a change on next login.

newPassword.description: Must satisfy the password policy (min 8 chars).

get.description: Container/liveness probe keyed on the background worker heartbeat: 200 while the heartbeat is fresh (≤ 3 min), 503 with the SAME body shape when stale or absent.

heartbeatAgeMs.description: Milliseconds since the worker heartbeat; null when none was ever recorded.
Milliseconds since the worker heartbeat; null when none was ever recorded.

complete.description: True once the first-run wizard has been completed.

post.description: No request body. Idempotent. The endpoint is permanently unauthenticated — the reverse-proxy gate exempts `/api/first-run/*` even after setup completes, so calling it post-setup is harmless.

get.description: Returns the live OpenAPI 3.1 document generated from the registry. The response body shape is the full OpenAPI 3.1 object; only the top-level `openapi` version field is modelled here.
Static list: 1=ebook, 2=audiobook, 3=light_novel, 4=manga, 5=comic.

get.summary: Media roots, one per content type
Authors (= bookkeeprr series), all 5 content types
get.description: Returns up to 500 series, title-ascending, each with its books (volumes).

authorName.description: Author; comics fall back to the publisher.

post.description: `metadataProfileId` selects the content type; `foreignAuthorId` is the provider id (see the body schema). 409 when the series already exists.

foreignAuthorId.description: Provider id per content type: openlibrary_id (ebook), asin (audiobook), anilist_id (light_novel/manga), mangadex_id (manga fallback), comicvine_id (comic).

authorName.description: Author; comics fall back to the publisher.
Author; comics fall back to the publisher.
Author; comics fall back to the publisher.

delete.summary: Delete the bookkeeprr series (files on disk untouched)

get.description: Searches all five metadata providers; results are not yet in the library.

post.description: Creates a series with one volume. 409 when a series with the same provider id already exists.

delete.summary: Delete the volume; the series stays

authorId.description: Synthetic 1-based result index, not a series id.

get.summary: Recent jobs in Readarr command shape (last 50)

post.summary: Dispatch a Readarr command to bookkeeprr’s job kinds
post.description: Always 201. Refresh*/…Search (with `authorId`) and RescanFolders enqueue a job — the record’s `id` is the bookkeeprr jobId and `message` the job kind; anything else (including an empty body) returns a synthetic `completed` no-op record with `id` 0.

name.description: RefreshAuthor / RefreshBook / RefreshAuthors / AuthorSearch / BookSearch / MissingBookSearch / RescanFolders; anything else is accepted as a no-op.

authorId.description: bookkeeprr series id; required for the Refresh*/…Search commands to enqueue.

get.description: Downloads with status != imported/superseded. Invalid pagination params fall back to the defaults rather than erroring.
Union of grabbed / bookFileImported / downloadFailed events, newest first, capped at the most recent 1000.

sourceTitle.description: Release title; the file path for imports.

get.description: Always `[]` — Readarr clients only check the shape.

bearerAuth.description: Personal API key (`Authorization: Bearer bkr_…`) created under Account → API keys. Either this or `X-Api-Key` is sufficient — send one, not both. Mobile app tokens issued by the mobile onboarding exchange use the same header.

apiKeyAuth.name: X-Api-Key
apiKeyAuth.description: Static API key generated under Settings → API Access. Either this or the bearer token is sufficient — send one, not both. The conventional mode for Readarr-compat clients.
